§6Research Reactor§f

§aA compact fissile research reactor for efficient fuel burning and neutron flux generation — the heart of any breeding setup!§f

The §bResearch Reactor§f is a non-power reactor intended for burning fuel efficiently and acting as a neutron source for breeding reactors. It must be §esubmerged in water§f since it lacks active cooling.

§6Construction§f (Assembly Machine — 10s, 100 HE/t):
§b• 8×§f Steel Ingot
§b• 4×§f Cadmium Steel Ingot
§b• 2×§f Desh Motor
§b• 5×§f Boron Ingot
§b• 8×§f Lead Plate
§b• 3×§f Cathode Ray Tube
§b• 4×§f Integrated Circuit Board

§6Operation§f:
• Requires a §bneutron source§f (Ra226Be or Pu238Be plate) to initiate reaction — can be removed after startup
• §e12 fuel plate slots§f split into vertical and horizontal sections (isolated from each other)
• Explodes at §e1,000°C§f — use caution with strong fuels
• Emits §bCherenkov radiation§f and creates oxyhydrogen bubbles

§6GUI Displays§f:
§b• Top Left§f — Flux (higher = better breeding)
§b• Middle Left§f — Heat (lower = better)
§b• Bottom Left§f — Control rod extraction % (100 = full power, 0 = off)

§6Breeding§f: Breeder reactors use total flux from the research reactor. Each recipe has a minimum flux requirement. Multiple reactors can feed one breeder. Shut down when not actively breeding to conserve expensive fuel.

§eSee Research Reactor Fuels & Breeding quest for detailed fuel stats, plate fuel crafting, and advanced breeding strategies!
